edges.note: GIF first-frame requirement is the email-specific case of the general motion/fallback rule.
Audience-confirmation requirement drives which reference-language variant applies.
Adjacent-dark enforcement relies on declared background metadata.
Declared footnote symbols feed assembly-time assignment.
Both define the modular section contract.
Emphasis-color reservation refines the general chart color binding.
Approved tint set includes the campaign headline letter tint.
Approved tint set includes the callout box fill tints.
Approved tint set includes the wave top layer transparency.
The masthead-with-logo rule selects one specific gradient from the approved set.
Horizontal direction governs the same masthead/banner gradient application.
Safe/forbidden pairs operationalize the WCAG contrast minimums.
Headline is part of the gradient callout section layout.
Button is part of the gradient callout section layout.
Signature radius further constrains the button treatment.
Asset-lock and skip conditions refine the section background rule.
Geometry rule specifies exact placement of the badge defined in the base structure rule.
Fallback handles clients that cannot honor the absolute-positioned overhang geometry.
Cardinality limit governs the styled primary CTA button.
Both are section padding rules supporting zero-gap assembly.
Spacing rhythm and canvas dimensions jointly define seamless email assembly.
Usage guidance builds on the wave device construction definition.
Warm lifestyle photography is housed within the accent-shape crop.
Wave/gradient overlays apply to the photography treatment.
Consistency constraint refines the base accent-shape device.
Charts/tables override the asymmetric accent shape with uniform corners.
Both define the chart/infographic system.
Container and header treatment co-apply within charts.
Stat callout hierarchy and footnote separation together establish data hierarchy.
Palette constraint applies alongside the minimal line style for all icons.
Minimum render size accompanies the base icon style rule.
Declared background metadata is what lets assemblers avoid identical adjacent backgrounds.
Both govern how the LISRAYA name is written; ALL CAPS applies to the same token that the trademark rule qualifies.
Body level in the scale is bounded by the 16px minimum.
Line-height minimums apply to the body level of the scale.
CSS stack operationalizes the Nunito Sans digital family for email.
The scale supports the required distinct-level hierarchy.

Chart/infographic headlines are a sanctioned exception to the no-all-caps rule.
Sentence-case body reinforces the no-all-caps-for-body prohibition.
Both govern emphasis usage; the device cap constrains how bold combines with other emphasis.
Together they define the permitted emphasis toolkit for patient copy.
Heading prohibition constrains where the DM abbreviation may appear.
Assembly rule extends the first-mention pattern to each standalone section.
